description_long: This object is a small, vintage-style biplane with a wooden fuselage and wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft. The main body is constructed from a brown, wood-textured material, with two stacked wings connected by struts and wires. The nose features a prominent, cylindrical engine cowling in a reddish-brown color, with a visible propeller hub but no propeller blades attached. The tail section is slender, ending in a conventional tail with a vertical stabilizer and two angled horizontal stabilizers. The undercarriage consists of a simple, fixed landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and a single seat visible.
description: A small, vintage wooden biplane with stacked wings, a reddish-brown engine cowling, and an open single-seat cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden fuselage, stacked wings, exposed engine cowling, and tail section with angled stabilizers.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the biplane's dual wing structure, narrow fuselage, and symmetrical tail design.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the landing gear and tail skid more visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the round engine cowling, open cockpit behind the upper wing, and the biplane's symmetrical wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylindrical cowling.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two angled horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage, with a small windscreen.
